author: anon-ab6221f8
content:
Plan — Phase 5.3 polish scope:

Three features, unified on a new /settings/preferences page:

1. Theme toggle — light/dark/system (follow media query). Palette lives in index.css; light mode added as a class override. Persisted to localStorage via a tiny module-level store with a subscribe() hook. Also exposed as a one-click toggle button in the top nav.

2. Audible alerts — plays a short WebAudio tone when a dashboard_alerts_changed WS event arrives with opened > 0. Severity-filtered (critical always, warning optional, info never by default). No external MP3 — oscillator-synthesized tones to keep the bundle small and avoid asset-pipeline fuss. Toggle + severity filter exposed on the preferences page; disabled by default (opt-in).

3. Preferences page — the unifying UI. Theme radio + audible toggle + severity checkboxes. No 'custom dashboards' tile-drag thing (that's design-doc-vague and a rabbit hole; scoped out explicitly).

Mechanics:
- lib/preferences.ts: typed localStorage-backed store + React subscription hook (useSyncExternalStore). Single source of truth.
- lib/theme.ts: applies theme class to documentElement on change + media-query listener for system mode.
- lib/alertSound.ts: WebAudio oscillator player + subscribe-to-WS helper.
- api/ws.ts: add a module-level event bus for alert events so non-queryClient consumers can react.
- pages/SettingsPreferences.tsx: the page.
- App.tsx: mount a ThemeBootstrap + AlertSound bridge, add nav + route.

Tests:
- preferences store roundtrip (vitest)
- useTheme applies the right class and listens to system changes
- alertSound plays on WS event that matches severity filter; stays silent otherwise
- SettingsPreferences renders all controls, toggles persist
- No backend changes this phase — pure frontend polish.

Deferred: custom dashboard tile layouts (too vague; would want a separate design pass), per-project mute, notification-snooze windows.
